theme.rationale: For an emergency pediatric reference, absolute clarity, data legibility, and function-first design are paramount. The Swiss & High-Contrast archetype provides the necessary authority and scannability under high-pressure situations.

mono_data.usage: MUST be used for all numerical values, dose amounts, weights, and formulas to ensure precise tabular alignment and readability (e.g., distinguishing 1, I, l, 0, O).

brand_primary.usage: Used sparingly for extreme critical alerts (e.g., Defibrillation, Cardiac Arrest). Do not overuse.

brand_secondary.usage: Used for standard CTA, selected tabs, active states.

grid_system.usage: Use this dense grid for the Live Dose Cards to ensure maximum visibility of drugs on a single screen without scrolling.

spacing_scale.rule: Generous spacing between major sections, but tight spacing within data cards.

surfaces.top_bar: Sticky/Fixed top, z-50, crystal glassmorphism: backdrop-blur-xl bg-white/80 dark:bg-black/80, 1px bottom border border-slate-200 dark:border-slate-800.
surfaces.dose_cards: Flat solid background, 1px subtle border, rounded-md (max 8px radius). No heavy shadows. Use drug_categories colors for left border accent (e.g. border-l-4).
surfaces.data_tables: border-collapse, alternating row colors (zebra striping) using very subtle grays (e.g., bg-slate-50 dark:bg-slate-900) for scannability.

top_bar.slider_rules: Must be thick (h-4 or h-6 track) for easy touch targeting. Thumb must be prominent. Real-time value updates.

tabs.style: Full width, scrollable on mobile. Active tab must have high contrast (e.g., solid brand_secondary background or thick underline).

broselow_tape.style: A horizontal bar split into 9 proportional color blocks. Each block is clickable to auto-set the weight slider to the median of that zone. Use precise hex codes from colors.broselow_tape.

algorithms.style: Flowchart style step cards. Use arrows (Phosphor icons) between steps. Differentiate 'Decision' nodes (diamond or outlined) from 'Action' nodes (solid fills).

icons_and_media.usage: Dense dashboards require Phosphor icons. Use regular or bold weights.

images.policy: NO IMAGES. Do not use tags, placeholders, or dummy images. The application is a clinical tool; rely strictly on typography, color-coding, and SVG icons.

motion_and_interactions.hover: Slight border darkening or subtle background tint change. No dramatic scale transformations. Focus on speed.
motion_and_interactions.focus: All inputs/buttons MUST have a highly visible focus ring: focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand_secondary focus:outline-none focus:ring-offset-2 dark:focus:ring-offset-black
motion_and_interactions.active: Press states should be immediate: active:scale-[0.98] transition-transform duration-75

accessibility_and_testing.contrast: Text must pass WCAG AA (4.5:1). Dark mode MUST be tested for readable contrast on Broselow colors.

test_ids.rule: All interactive elements MUST include data-testid attribute.

instructions_to_main_agent: Prioritize load speed and instantaneous reactivity of the weight slider modifying all dose cards.
Implement the Broselow Tape as a visual index that also functions as quick-nav (clicking 'Purple' sets weight to ~10.5kg).
Ensure the Equipment Tube Table is fully responsive; wrap in overflow-x-auto for mobile devices.
For the Algorithm flowcharts, build semantic HTML cards connected by CSS or simple SVG lines, do not embed static images.
For PDF export, use a library like 'react-to-pdf' or 'jspdf'. Ensure the exported document uses a clean, white background regardless of the user's active theme.
Strictly follow the color mappings for drug categories to allow clinicians to rapidly distinguish sedation from resuscitation meds visually.
Always use JetBrains Mono for numbers to avoid misreading critical doses.
